mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity prices adjust to higher expected corporate revenues.
failure_condition: The strategy produces positive 20-day forward returns in less than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or the Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.5% indicates a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through higher expected earnings.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate and an increase in retail sales identifies a reinforcing recovery in labor income and consumer expenditures, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE MoM decline coincident with RSAFS MoM increase
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE falls and RSAFS rises, the S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the interaction term exhibits a t-statistic below 1.5 in a forward-return regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals reduced future liquidity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, months with FEDFUNDS increases produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases, or the mean forward return is insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals a positive shock to aggregate supply and manufacturing corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets rapidly price real-economy momentum.
failure_condition: Months with rising INDPRO fail to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or the Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP signals broad-based demand expansion that lifts expected corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the equity market gradually discounts the sustained output growth.
failure_condition: Quarters with rising GDPC1 produce positive 60-day forward returns in less than 55% of instances, or the information coefficient over any 5-year window falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in consumer prices and an unchanged or lower federal funds rate produces a negative real interest rate shock that stimulates leveraged corporate investment and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is flat or falls
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a predictive regression.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts above 1,100,000 units signal above-trend residential investment and durable construction demand that drives upstream commodity and employment growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1100, the S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the conditional mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
